# docsfy

> AI-powered documentation site generator that creates polished, Mintlify-quality static HTML docs from GitHub repositories

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](introduction.md): Overview of docsfy, what it does, how it uses AI CLI tools to generate beautiful documentation sites from GitHub repositories, and the core value proposition
- [Quickstart](quickstart.md): Step-by-step guide to get docsfy running locally with Docker Compose, generate your first documentation site, and view it in the browser
- [Installation](installation.md): Detailed installation options including Docker, docker-compose, and local development setup with uv and Python 3.12+

## Core Concepts

- [Architecture](architecture.md): High-level architecture of the FastAPI server, the four-stage generation pipeline, storage layout, and how components interact
- [Generation Pipeline](generation-pipeline.md): Deep dive into the four sequential stages: repository cloning, AI-powered planning, concurrent content generation, and HTML rendering
- [AI Providers](ai-providers.md): Supported AI CLI providers (Claude Code, Cursor Agent, Gemini CLI), their command interfaces, CWD handling, and how provider selection works
- [Incremental Updates](incremental-updates.md): How docsfy tracks commit SHAs, detects repository changes, and selectively regenerates only affected documentation pages

## Configuration

- [Environment Variables](environment-variables.md): Complete reference for all environment variables including AI_PROVIDER, AI_MODEL, AI_CLI_TIMEOUT, provider-specific API keys, and logging settings
- [AI Provider Setup](ai-provider-setup.md): How to configure credentials for each AI provider: Anthropic API key, Google Cloud Vertex AI, Gemini API key, and Cursor API key
- [Docker Configuration](docker-configuration.md): Dockerfile details, docker-compose.yaml configuration, volume mounts for persistent storage and credentials, health checks, and port mapping

## Usage

- [Generating Documentation](generating-docs.md): How to submit a repository for documentation generation via the POST /api/generate endpoint, monitor progress, and access the result
- [Serving Documentation](serving-docs.md): Accessing generated documentation through the built-in static file server at /docs/{project}/ or downloading as a tar.gz archive for self-hosting
- [Managing Projects](managing-projects.md): Listing projects with status, viewing project details including last generated timestamp and commit SHA, and deleting projects

## API Reference

- [POST /api/generate](api-generate.md): Start documentation generation for a GitHub repository URL, including request format, response structure, and status tracking
- [GET /api/status](api-status.md): List all projects and their current generation status (generating, ready, or error)
- [GET /api/projects/{name}](api-projects.md): Retrieve detailed project information including last generated timestamp, commit SHA, and page listing
- [DELETE /api/projects/{name}](api-projects-delete.md): Remove a project and all its generated documentation from storage
- [GET /api/projects/{name}/download](api-download.md): Download the complete generated documentation site as a .tar.gz archive for self-hosting
- [GET /docs/{project}/{path}](api-docs-serving.md): Serve generated static HTML documentation pages directly from the docsfy server
- [GET /health](api-health.md): Health check endpoint for container orchestration and monitoring

## Generated Sites

- [HTML Theme & Features](html-theme.md): Overview of the generated site features: sidebar navigation, dark/light theme toggle, client-side search with lunr.js, code syntax highlighting, card layouts, callout boxes, and responsive design
- [Plan JSON Schema](plan-json-schema.md): Structure and schema of the plan.json file produced by the AI Planner, including pages, sections, and navigation hierarchy
- [Customization](customization.md): How the Jinja2 templates, bundled CSS/JS assets, and site structure can be customized for different documentation needs

## Development

- [Development Setup](development-setup.md): Setting up a local development environment with uv, pre-commit hooks, and the development toolchain
- [Project Structure](project-structure.md): Layout of the docsfy codebase including source modules, templates, assets, tests, and configuration files
- [Testing](testing.md): Running the test suite with tox, unit tests via uv, unused-code checks, and quality gates
- [Code Quality](code-quality.md): Pre-commit hooks configuration including ruff linting and formatting, mypy strict type checking, flake8, gitleaks, and detect-secrets
